data.title: SpaceX Stock Bulls vs. Bears — Is the Nearly 50% Drop a Buying Opportunity or a Warning?
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) stock has had a rough ride since its blockbuster IPO. The shares climbed as high as $225.64 soon after their debut but now trade near $118, a drop of nearly 50% from that peak. Yet Wall Street remains bullish, with the average price target pointing to more than 100% upside.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

So, has the steep drop created a buying opportunity, or does SPCX stock still face more downside? Here's a look at both sides.

Why SpaceX Bulls Still See Big Upside

Bulls see several businesses that could drive SpaceX's growth for years. The company is a leader in rocket launches, and its reusable rockets help cut the cost of sending satellites and other cargo into space.

SpaceX also runs Starlink, a network of satellites that provides high-speed internet to homes and businesses. Starlink has grown into a major part of the company's business and gives SpaceX another source of revenue beyond rocket launches.

Then there is AI. SpaceX is spending heavily on AI computing and has much bigger plans for the future, including putting data centers in space. The idea is to use satellites to provide computing power from orbit. This is still a long-term plan, but bulls believe it could become a large new business.

Wall Street also sees plenty of upside. SPCX has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 23 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell. The average SpaceX price target of $243.81 implies about 106% upside from recent levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: AMD Stock: 4 Biggest Takeaways from AMD's Advancing AI 2026 Event
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) held its Advancing AI 2026 event in San Francisco on July 22–23, where CEO Lisa Su unveiled the company's latest AI products and strategy. The company also announced major partnerships with leading AI companies and raised its long-term AI market forecast.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Two ETFs for long or short leverage on CBRS

The announcements highlight AMD's strategy to challenge Nvidia (NVDA) in the fast-growing AI infrastructure market as demand shifts from AI training to inference and agentic AI. Here are the biggest takeaways investors should know.

AMD Launches Next-Generation AI Platform

AMD introduced Helios, its newest rack-scale AI platform, powered by AMD's latest AI GPUs and EPYC server processors. According to AMD, Helios delivers up to 30% more tokens per dollar than competing AI systems, making it more cost-efficient for customers.

The company said Helios is already in production and will begin shipping in the third quarter. AMD also reaffirmed its commitment to annual AI hardware launches through 2030, including the MI500 and MI600 GPU families.

OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta Deepen Partnerships

One of the biggest highlights was that several leading AI companies are adopting AMD's technology.

Anthropic plans to deploy up to two gigawatts of AMD's MI455X GPUs through Helios systems and will work with AMD to improve its ROCm AI software using Claude.

OpenAI is partnering with AMD to optimize GPT models on Helios systems, with deployments expected to begin in the fourth quarter of 2026 and accelerate throughout 2027.

Meanwhile, Meta (META) has begun validating AMD's latest processors and testing Helios systems ahead of broader deployments.

AMD also announced new collaborations with AT&T (T), Cisco (CSCO), and Cerebras (CBRS) to expand enterprise AI adoption.

AMD Sees a Much Bigger AI Market Ahead

CEO Su said AMD now expects the AI accelerator market to reach $1.4 trillion by 2030, nearly triple the company's estimate from a year ago.

AMD also forecast the server CPU market will exceed $200 billion by the end of the decade. The company noted that AI inference has now become the industry's largest compute workload, overtaking AI training as more companies deploy AI applications in real-world use.

AMD Continues Investing in AI Software

Beyond hardware, AMD continued expanding its ROCm software ecosystem, its open-source alternative to Nvidia's CUDA platform. AMD also introduced ROCm AI, a software platform that helps developers build AI applications more easily using AI coding tools like Claude and Codex. The company is expanding its software tools to make it easier for businesses to adopt AMD's AI chips.

What Investors Should Watch

The biggest takeaway from AMD's event was that major AI companies, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta, are increasingly adopting its technology. These partnerships could help AMD gain market share in AI infrastructure, where Nvidia remains the dominant player.

Investors will now be watching whether these partnerships and product launches translate into faster AI revenue growth over the coming quarters. AMD's expanded roadmap and higher AI market forecast also reinforce management's view that AI infrastructure spending remains in the early stages of a long-term growth cycle.

Is AMD a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, AMD has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 28 Buys and eight Hold ratings. The average Advanced Micro Devices price target of $541.31 implies that shares are almost fully valued at current levels. Year-to-date, AMD shares have surged 152%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Bloom Energy Stock (BE): Why Is the Panama Deal Buzz Not Exciting Investors?
data.description:
Shares in Bloom Energy (BE), the solid-oxide fuel cell systems developer, struggled to eke out a 1% gain early Friday. This came despite speculation of a new deployment deal in Panama. The lack of excitement comes as BE stock has tumbled by over 10% over the past five days and about 33% over the last month. This was triggered by short-seller attacks and worries about a deployment delay in a top project by Oracle (ORCL).

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Two ETFs for long or short leverage on BE

Is Bloom Tied to New Massive Data Center Projects in Panama?

On Thursday, financial media Core Finance published an interview with Charlie Faulkner, the CEO of data center developer EdgeMode (EDGM).

In the interview, Faulkner revealed that the company has expanded its portfolio with two new 1.2GW projects in Panama. The chief executive noted that the two new projects build on its push to expand its capacity in Europe using solid-oxide fuel cell technology.

In response, RBC Capital analyst Chris Dendrinos noted that Bloom is the likely power supplier for the two projects. He believes the projects could be an extension of Bloom's existing partnership with EdgeMode.

Why RBC Capital Remains Bullish on Bloom

According to Dendrinos, the potential deal indicates that demand for Bloom's product remains strong. As a result, the analyst reaffirmed his Buy rating on Bloom's shares.

In addition, Dendrinos kept his $335 price target on BE stock. This implies about 54% upside ahead.

The new rating comes days after JPMorgan (JPM) also maintained its Buy rating on Bloom shares. The banking giant pointed to the rising demand for power. Also, it noted that the recent sell-off in clean energy and power stocks had opened "compelling entry points."

Meanwhile, Bloom secured a new $1.7 billion investment in its technology last week.

Is Bloom Energy a Good Stock to Buy?

On Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Bloom Energy's shares. This is based on nine Buys and 11 Holds issued over the past three months.

However, the average BE price target of $289.40 implies about 33% upside (see BE stock forecast here).
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 7/24/26 – Futures Edge Higher Despite Elevated Oil Prices
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher ahead of Friday's session after a sharp selloff on Wall Street, as investors weighed elevated oil prices and escalating U.S.-Iran tensions. Brent crude briefly climbed above $100 a barrel on Thursday for the first time in two months before retreating, fueling concerns about potential oil supply disruptions.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.11%, 0.23%, and 0.50%, respectively, at 5:33 a.m. EDT on July 24.

Oil prices were set for weekly gains after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea raised concerns about disruptions to another important oil shipping route. Kazakhstan's temporary production cuts also supported prices. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 2.68% at $93.97 a barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) slipped 3.39% at $89.66 a barrel.

On Thursday, major U.S. stock indices fell sharply as investors worried about rising AI spending following earnings reports from Tesla (TSLA) and Alphabet (GOOGL). The Nasdaq dropped 2.15%, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.21% and 0.97%, respectively. The major indices are now on track for weekly losses, with the Dow, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq down 0.8%, 0.7%, and 1.5%, respectively.

The benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield stood at 4.68% as of the last check. Additionally, the spot gold price in U.S. dollars traded around $4,053.90 per ounce on Friday.

On the earnings front, Verizon (VZ), American Express (AXP), NextEra Energy (NEE), and SLB (SLB) are scheduled to report results today.

Elsewhere, European markets opened higher today as investors reacted to the region's corporate earnings reports.

Asia-Pacific Markets Close Lower

Asia-Pacific markets closed lower after U.S. President Donald Trump imposed a 12.5% tariff on imports from Australia, China, Hong Kong, Singapore, and South Korea. The U.S. said the countries had failed to prevent goods made with forced labor from entering the American market.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.98%. In China, the Shanghai Composite slumped 1.61%, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 2.85%. In Japan, the Nikkei declined 2.73%, while the Topix lost 1.05%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: SoftBank Building Humanoid Foundations in Proposed $500M Robotic Diggers Deal
data.description:
Japanese investor SoftBank Group (SFTBY) is reportedly eyeing an acquisition of robot digger-maker Gravis Robotics as it continues to build a strong presence in the new world of humanoids and automation.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Automated Earthmoving Equipment

According to an article in Bloomberg, SoftBank is planning to build a large interest in the Zurich-based start-up and hold it in a new company it is creating called Roze. It is understood that SoftBank may look to carry out the Gravis deal in several stages by buying existing shares and injecting fresh capital over time.

Founded in 2022, Gravis makes hardware and software that helps earthmoving equipment operate autonomously. The company raised $23 million in venture funding last November.

Gravis' range of robotic technology includes the Gravis RACK for use with construction machines. It creates automated excavators by using sensors and an intuitive touchscreen control to "augment your existing crew for safer sites and superhuman productivity."

As reported by Bloomberg, a deal could ultimately value Gravis at over $500 million, however it is understood that no final decisions on the size or the structure of the deal have been reached.

European Robotics Push

It is not SoftBank's first foray into the world of European robotics. SoftBank has long seen the potential of the humanoid race. It agreed to buy Swiss conglomerate ABB's (ABBNY) industrial robots unit last year for $5.4 billion.

Back in June it was reported that SoftBank was ready to be part of an $800 million funding round for Germany's Agile Robots.

It was understood then that SoftBank was in talks to contribute over $300 million to Agile, whose Physical AI solutions include the Agile ONE robot. It is marketed as having "unprecedentedly dexterous hands, advanced perception, and intuitive human interaction" for industrial settings. It also has the Diana7, which is a robot arm powered by AI and AgileCore, its AI software platform.

SoftBank is also planning to create and list an AI and robotics company called Roze in the U.S. It could be listed as early as this year, with executives at SoftBank eyeing a valuation of up to $100 billion.

China dominates the humanoid market which, according to Goldman Sachs, could grow from about $3 billion in 2023 to as much as $38 billion by 2035. Global investment in robotic startups has also more than tripled from a year earlier to $27.6 billion in 2025, according to data from PitchBook.

What are the Best Robotics Stocks to Buy Now?

We have rounded up the best Robotics stocks to buy now using our TipRanks Stock Comparison tool. As can be seen below, Serve Robotics (SERV) has the best upside of 307.14%.
data.author: David Craik

data.description:
Salesforce (CRM) stock rose about 1.5% in Friday's pre-market trading after a major new win. The company secured a $1.6 billion contract with the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). Salesforce will help the VA improve its care and services using its data, cloud, and AI tools.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

For context, Salesforce is a major cloud software company. Its tools help businesses and government agencies manage customer data, sales, and other tasks.

Salesforce Adds Another Major U.S. Contract

The agreement was secured through Missionforce, which serves U.S. agencies. It runs for one year, with options for two more years.

Under the plan, the VA will use Salesforce's tools to update its systems and improve how it serves veterans.

The latest award follows other large wins for Missionforce. These include a $5.6 billion U.S. Army contract and a $72 million Air Force deal. The new VA award could help Salesforce grow its federal business at a time when investors want to see more gains from its AI push.

CRM Stock Price Performance

Salesforce stock has been under pressure in recent weeks and has traded close to its 52-week low. The stock has lost 40% so far this year. A recent Morgan Stanley downgrade also weighed on sentiment. The firm's analyst Adam Wood cut CRM to Equal Weight, citing slower-than-expected growth from Agentforce, Salesforce's AI agent platform for businesses. He wants to see clearer signs that the platform can help boost sales growth.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Is Oracle Stock Rising in Pre-Market Today, July 24?
data.description:
Shares of Oracle (ORCL) are up by 3% in pre-market trading on Friday, recovering from a 4.6% drop in the previous session. The gain came after the U.S. Department of Defense awarded Oracle a software contract worth up to $7 billion over 10 years.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Explore ORCZ for 2X short leverage on ORCL

For context, Oracle is a U.S.-based technology company best known for its database software and cloud computing services.

More Details About the Contract

The contract begins with a five-year term worth up to $3.31 billion. It also includes an option to extend for another five years, bringing the total value to nearly $7 billion. The deal includes the use of Oracle software, maintenance, support, cloud services, and consulting for the Pentagon, the Coast Guard, and U.S. intelligence agencies.

The Department of Defense said the new deal will cut costs by at least $441 million by improving how it purchases Oracle's on-premises software and services.

For investors, the deal highlights that Oracle's software is still widely used by major government agencies. That helps support steady revenue while its cloud business continues to grow.

ORCL Stock Performance

The contract is positive for Oracle, but the company still faces some big challenges. Oracle stock is down over 35% year-to-date as investors worry about the high cost of its AI expansion.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: 'It's Waiting Time,' Says Investor About SpaceX Stock
data.description:
It is no secret that investor sentiment can shift quickly on Wall Street, and SpaceX (NASDAQ:SPCX) currently provides a good example. After delivering a record-breaking IPO last month, the upcoming August 6 lock-up expiry, an aborted Starship test and concerns about the stock's demanding valuation, have all contributed to the change in mood.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

With the stock now sitting 41% below last month's highs, the question is whether the sell-off has created an opportunity to buy in. Not according to an investor who goes by the name of Esxeleryn Analytics.

Esxeleryn argues that SpaceX is attempting to execute on several highly ambitious, capital-intensive and largely unproven initiatives at the same time. That leaves little room for execution errors. The company's forward EV/EBITDA multiple is approximately 93.6x, while its forward EV/Sales multiple is around 40.7x.

The risks could become particularly acute if the AI infrastructure spending boom loses momentum, as the Bank for International Settlements (BIS) has warned could happen. Further delays at Terafab, the company's planned semiconductor fabrication plant, could also create problems, particularly if the project experiences setbacks similar to those encountered by TSMC with its Arizona fabs.

In that scenario, SpaceX could find itself facing a "liquidity crisis." The company's significant capital expenditure requirements mean that cash burn could remain substantial, potentially forcing it to return to the capital markets regularly to fund its expansion.

That funding could become progressively more expensive. The bond market is already demanding elevated yields, while the number of shares available to trade could increase significantly as lock-up restrictions expire. Without the benefit of S&P 500 inclusion and the associated passive fund demand, SpaceX may have to contend with a rapidly rising cost of capital.

Esxeleryn also believes that the current public float does not accurately reflect the eventual supply of shares available to investors. As additional shares become available following lock-up expirations, the resulting increase in supply could trigger a further valuation reset.

The investor believes this could lead Wall Street to re-rate the stock materially lower, potentially forcing the stock's valuation closer to the multiples typically associated with more mature aerospace and telecom hardware businesses. Retail investors and late-stage institutional buyers could be particularly vulnerable, having potentially bought shares at elevated prices while providing an exit opportunity for pre-IPO venture investors.

So, what is the best course of action?

"It is better to wait and observe SPCX stock's premium to go away," Esxeleryn said. "This price free fall may provide smart bulls with a de-risked entry point to capitalize on SpaceX's business model shift from a hardware launch provider into a high-margin vertically integrated AI Neocloud infrastructure leader. Selling now is too late and should be avoided against TAM, the Terafab and Cursor integrations scale, and progressive business model tilt."

Bottom line, Esxeleryn rates SPCX stock a Hold (i.e., Neutral). (To watch Esxeleryn Analytics' track record, click here)

Overall, the Street is far more bullish. While 5 analysts also stay on the sidelines, with an additional 23 Buys and 1 Sell, the stock claims a Strong Buy consensus rating. Meanwhile, the $243.81 average price target offers 12-month upside of 106%. (See SPCX stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: AMD Stock Gets New Street-High Price Target Among Several Hikes – Here's Why
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices' (NASDAQ:AMD) 2026 Advancing AI event appears to have made a big impression on Wall Street, with a host of Wall Street analysts raising their price targets following the latest announcements.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

At the event, AMD said its rack-scale AI platform, Helios, has entered full production. Shipments are expected to begin in September, with the rollout accelerating through the first half of 2027. The company identified Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, Microsoft and Oracle as its initial customers.

AMD also substantially increased its outlook for the server CPU market, projecting growth from approximately $26 billion in 2025 to $220 billion by 2030. The company said the expansion will be driven by agentic AI workloads, which require significantly greater infrastructure to support orchestration, tool calling, data access and inference management. The new estimate is roughly $100 billion higher than AMD's previous projection of a server CPU TAM exceeding $120 billion by 2030, issued during its Q1 earnings call. It also slightly surpasses Nvidia's latest $200 billion estimate for the market.

Among analysts taking it all in, Baird's Tristan Gerra, ranked among the top 1% of Street stock pros, appeared to be the most bullish of the lot.

Gerra reiterated an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating and raised his price target from $625 to a Street-high of $1,250, suggesting the stock will gain 132% over the coming year. "Raising our estimates to reflect strong growth in both AMD's AI GPU and AI CPU platforms. We are now modeling $147B in AI GPU revenue by 2030, assuming 15% market share," the 5-star analyst said. (To watch Gerra's track record, click here)

Meanwhile, UBS's Timothy Arcuri, ranked 2nd among the thousands of Street analysts, maintained a Buy rating on the shares and raised his price target from $700 to $730, implying 12-month upside of 35% from current levels. (To watch Arcuri's track record, click here)

Arcuri's "new view" is based on the possibility that AMD's 2028 EPS could approach $30, following the company's latest commentary on its CPU roadmap, market share ambitions and TAM at its AI Day. "We believe server CPU is going to grow so fast and, with gross margin that we think is ~1,000bps above corporate average, this could establish a margin "umbrella" under which AMD can price an impressively spec'd MI500 on the aggressive side in 2027 to take incrementally more share of the GPU TAM," the 5-star analyst went on to say.

Arcuri also said TSMC's recent increase in capital spending had improved his confidence in AMD's supply outlook, while server CPU revenue could reach approximately $35 billion in 2027 and potentially $60 billion in 2028. "AMD remains a favorite idea even after a ~75% move in the past 3 months," he summed up.

Melius analyst Ben Reitzes is just as effusive. Although AMD is scheduled to report earnings in less than two weeks, Reitzes said the company had already "clearly signaled" that its 2027 and long-term revenue potential is likely higher than consensus expectations. With OpenAI and Meta on track, he also sees greater potential for Anthropic-related revenue in 2027 than previously anticipated. "CPUs are simply on fire as well and the amount of revenue AMD plans to capture from agents is higher than expected," Reitzes said.

While his estimates are likely to rise following AMD's August 4 earnings report, the revenue growth CAGR of more than 35% through 2030 outlined at the November Analyst Day now appears "too conservative."

Accordingly, Reitzes reiterated a Buy rating on the shares and raised his price target from $540 to $660, implying the stock will deliver returns of 22% a year from now. (To watch Reitzes' track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, the stock claims an additional 25 Buys and 8 Holds, for a Strong Buy consensus rating. At $569.44, the average price target suggests shares will add 5.5% in the months ahead. (See AMD stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Why Are Micron and SanDisk Stocks Falling in Pre-Market Today Despite Intel's Strong Earnings?
data.description:
Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) stocks moved lower in Friday's pre-market trading, even as Intel (INTC) surged after strong second-quarter results. The weakness in MU and SNDK comes as memory stocks remain under pressure after their recent pullback.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

SNDK has a long & short ETF? Explore SNXX & SNDQ

The moves also follow a sharp sell-off in South Korean chip stocks. The KOSPI fell over 6%, while Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and SK Hynix (SKHY) dropped more than 7%. The steep losses also triggered trading halts in Seoul. This weakness appears to have spilled into U.S. memory stocks, putting pressure on Micron and SanDisk despite Intel's strong results.

Meanwhile, Intel stock jumped about 4% before the opening bell. Its strong results also lifted Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Arm Holdings (ARM), showing a clear split in Friday's early chip trading. At the same time, Micron fell nearly 2%, while SanDisk dropped over 3%.

Intel's Results Point to Strong AI Demand

Intel reported second-quarter revenue of $16.1 billion, up nearly 25% from last year. Adjusted EPS came in at $0.42, well above Wall Street's estimate of $0.22.

Its Data Center and AI sales jumped 59% to $6.3 billion. Intel also gave a better-than-expected outlook for the third quarter. The company expects revenue of $15.8 billion to $16.8 billion and EPS of $0.38.

The results point to solid demand for data-center chips. However, that strength has not been enough to lift all chip stocks today.

Why Are Memory Stocks Still Under Pressure?

Micron and SanDisk have seen sharp swings after a huge run in memory stocks. Investors have been weighing strong AI demand against concerns about high valuations and the risk of more chip supply over time. Still, Micron stock is up more than 240% in 2026, while SNDK has surged over 570% despite its recent pullback.

Still, Wall Street remains positive on the memory chip market. Five-star KeyBanc analyst John Vinh  expects memory supply to remain tight through 2027, which could support higher chip prices. This outlook could benefit memory chipmakers such as Micron and SanDisk.

Which Stock Does Wall Street See More Upside In?

Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we compared Micron and SanDisk to see which stock offers more upside based on analysts' price targets.

Both stocks carry Strong Buy consensus ratings. Analysts see more upside in Micron. The average MU price target of $1,569.29 implies 58.48% upside, while SanDisk's average price target of $2,052.50 implies 27.46% upside.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
